title: Today's AI Brief: Florida Sues OpenAI, Salesforce Unveils Slackbot AI
summary: Florida sues OpenAI over violent incidents, Salesforce rolls out new Slackbot AI agent
imageAlt: Image from source article for Florida sues OpenAI, Sam Altman, in first-of-its-kind lawsuit over violent incidents
imageCaption: Florida sues OpenAI, Sam Altman, in first-of-its-kind lawsuit over violent incidents
imageSourceTitle: Florida sues OpenAI, Sam Altman, in first-of-its-kind lawsuit over violent incidents

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to your AI Brief. I've got two interesting stories to share with you today. First up, Florida's attorney general has sued OpenAI and its CEO, Sam Altman, over alleged links to violent incidents involving ChatGPT.

The lawsuit claims ChatGPT was involved in a number of violent incidents, though details are scarce. What stands out to me is that this is a first-of-its-kind state litigation effort. It's a sign that governments are taking a closer look at the potential risks associated with AI.

Next, Salesforce has launched a rebuilt version of Slackbot, its workplace assistant. This new AI agent is capable of much more than just sending notifications. It can apparently do some actual work, which is great news for Slack users. Salesforce is competing with Microsoft and Google in the workplace AI space, and this move seems aimed at keeping pace.

I am looking at these developments and thinking about the bigger picture. As AI models get more powerful, we're seeing new questions about data boundaries, safety, and trust. It's interesting to see how companies are navigating these issues.

My read is that we're seeing a shift in how AI is being used and regulated. It's not just about building more powerful models; it's about making sure they're used in ways that benefit society. I'm curious to see how these stories play out and what other announcements we can expect in the coming weeks.

sources.title: Florida sues OpenAI, Sam Altman, in first-of-its-kind lawsuit over violent incidents
Salesforce rolls out new Slackbot AI agent as it battles Microsoft and Google in workplace AI
